overview: The Marathon County Board of Supervisors held an adjourned organizational meeting where they approved committee appointments, elected Supervisors Hapa and Arstead to the executive committee, and unanimously approved a resolution reducing landfill tipping fees for severe weather debris following the recent tornado and flooding disaster affecting Ringle and other communities. The board also approved several consent agenda items and budget resolutions related to out-of-home placement and social services.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Reading of the notice and roll call
Election of at-large executive committee members
Resolution approving out-of-home placement reserve increase (D15)
Resolution to amend social services budget (D16)
Resolution approving modification to landfill tipping fees (E17)
Vote on suspension of rules for tipping fee resolution

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs outlined goals for committee appointments including increasing diversity of perspectives, developing new supervisors as leaders, and accommodating supervisor preferences. Vice Chair Boots assisted in developing the appointments. The motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Crims, was carried but not unanimous. Over 34% of the board is in its first or second term.

discussions.item: Election of At-Large Executive Committee Members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated for two at-large executive committee positions: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Vice Chair Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg explained she nominated Supervisor Leur because she 'advocates for our county's smallest children and our oldest elders.' Paper ballots were distributed and Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the executive committee.

discussions.item: Landfill Tipping Fee Modification for Disaster Debris
discussions.body: Administrator Leonard and Solid Waste Director Hagen Busher presented a proposal to reduce tipping fees for debris from the recent tornado and flooding. The state waived $13.10 of fees under the emergency declaration, and Ringle waived their $2.33 fee. The county proposed reducing fees to $36.94 per ton (covering costs only) rather than the standard $65 gate rate. Two residents, Jessica Toasty and Matt Haybeck, were granted special dispensation to speak, asking the county to waive fees entirely. Toasty noted that the community has been 'very self-reliant' with businesses donating up to $40,000 in cleanup expenses. The board voted unanimously to suspend rules to consider the resolution directly, then unanimously approved Resolution 26-26 modifying the tipping fees through June 17th.
Items C10 through C14A were approved unanimously on a motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck.
Resolution 24-26 was approved on a motion by Supervisor Morash, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The motion carried but was not unanimous.
Resolution 25-26 was approved on a motion by Supervisor P, seconded by Supervisor Robinson. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

publicComment: Two residents were granted special permission to address the board regarding the landfill tipping fee resolution. Jessica Toasty asked the county to waive fees entirely for six months, noting that the state, DNR, and Ringle have waived their fees and that private businesses have donated significant resources for cleanup. Matt Haybeck spoke on behalf of residents whose homes must be demolished, asking for relief for underinsured homeowners and requesting more time for the fee waiver since some insurance adjusters haven't yet arrived.
actionItems: Committee appointments approved as presented by Chair Gibbs and Vice Chair Boots
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead elected to at-large executive committee positions
Resolution 26-26 approved reducing landfill tipping fees to $36.94 per ton for certified disaster debris through June 17th
Administrator Leonard to work with Community Foundation on mechanism for reimbursing uninsured debris disposal costs
Solid Waste Management Board to develop long-term policy on handling disaster-related fee reductions within six months
Resolution 24-26 approved for out-of-home placement reserve increase
Resolution 25-26 approved amending social services budget
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
